source_findings.raw_text: Human vs LLM prose naturalness ranking tasks using parallel prompts with concreteness and texture discriminators.
Cadence-only classification tasks distinguishing human vs LLM paragraphs using structural features.
Revision workflows comparing human drafts vs LLM revisions to detect voice erosion.
Human-like quality MUST be operationalized via multi-dimensional rubrics including coherence, continuity, dialogue realism, style consistency, and human-likeness discrimination.
Configure evaluators (LLM-judges, discriminators, metrics) to run with greedy decoding and fixed seeds to ensure deterministic behavior.
Periodically re-calibrate automated evaluators against human rubric benchmarks for both narrative and dialogue.
Use regression tests to block deployments where stability improves but agreement with human or reference metrics degrades.
Detector overfit to superficial artifacts misclassifies human text as AI or vice versa.
Stability metrics report "stable but wrong" patterns where outputs are consistent yet misaligned with references or human judgment.
Human vs AI discrimination – evaluate mixed corpus using discriminator_human_like_v1 and llm_judge_human_like_pairwise_v2; assert AUC and Elo within target ranges.
Dialogue realism – evaluate multi_turn_reference_dialogue_set_v1 with dialogue_automatic_metric_suite_v2 and human_dialogue_realism_rubric_v1 alignment checks.
Voice preservation after editing – measure voice_preservation_after_edit_v1 and semantic fidelity on edited_pairs_voice_labelled_v1; compare to human labels.

proposals.description: Propose a validation concept stack for human likeness discriminator stack.
proposals.rationale: The findings converge on multi-metric evaluation rather than single-score checking, with separate discriminators, validators, and scoring surfaces.
proposals.expected_impact: Creates a future validation portfolio that can support acceptance, regression detection, and comparative evaluation.
